description_long: This is a weathered, open-topped cardboard box with four flaps, two of which are partially folded outward and two standing more upright. The box is heavily stained and worn, with a mottled brown and black surface suggesting prolonged exposure to dirt or moisture. On one side, a 'FRAGILE' stamp is visible, accompanied by handling symbols, indicating it was used for shipping or storage of delicate items. The interior is empty and the flaps are irregularly bent, giving the box a slightly deformed appearance. The cardboard material appears thin and flexible, with no visible reinforcements or handles. The overall look suggests it has been discarded or left outdoors for some time.
description: A battered, open cardboard box with stained, mottled surfaces and bent flaps, featuring a 'FRAGILE' stamp on one side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 'FRAGILE' stamp, two upright flaps, and two flaps bent outward.
description_view_1: Side view with all four flaps visible, showing the box's depth and the worn, mottled texture.
description_view_2: Rear angled view highlighting the irregularly bent flaps and the box's empty interior.
description_view_3: Back view with all four flaps open, showing the box's rectangular shape and heavy surface staining.
